name: Marketing Blog Post
description: Turn a topic (or a research brief) into a publish-ready blog post with YAML frontmatter for mchatai.com/blog. Three LLM passes: outline, draft, polish + frontmatter.

config.userPrompt:
You are writing for the mChatAI blog. Audience: indie creators and prosumer Mac users who want to build games, tools, podcasts, and automations using AI. Voice: direct, helpful, no hype, no jargon. Skip phrases like 'in today's fast-paced world' or 'unlock the power of'.

Given the topic below, produce a detailed section-by-section outline.

Requirements:
- 4 to 6 main sections with clear H2 headings.
- 2 to 3 bullet sub-points per section noting what to cover.
- Open with a hook section that earns the reader's attention in two sentences.
- Close with a section that gives the reader a concrete next action.
- Return the outline only  -  no prose yet.

TOPIC:
{{INPUT}}

Expand this outline into a full blog post for the mChatAI blog.

Requirements:
- Write 2 to 4 paragraphs of engaging prose per section.
- Use the H2 headings from the outline. Use H3 sparingly.
- Tone: direct, helpful, conversational, ASCII only (no smart quotes, no em-dashes  -  use a regular hyphen).
- Open with a compelling 2-sentence hook, no preamble.
- Use markdown formatting.
- If you reference a specific mChatAI feature, name it plainly (Pipelines, AIWizard, Hub, Recipes, Skills, Agents, mChatAIShell).
- Do NOT invent stats, screenshots, or quotes.
- Do NOT include marketing fluff. If a sentence does not earn its place, cut it.
- End with a concrete call to action: which page on mchatai.com to visit next, or which recipe to try.

OUTLINE:
{{INPUT}}

Polish this blog post and prepend a YAML frontmatter block so it can be published to mchatai.com/blog. ASCII only  -  no smart quotes, no em-dashes, no curly apostrophes.

Return EXACTLY this format and nothing else:

---
slug: <kebab-case, max 60 chars, no stop words, derived from the title>
title: <title, max 70 chars, no clickbait>
summary: <one sentence, 120-200 chars, mirrors the post's hook>
author: mChatAI
publishedAt: <today's date as YYYY-MM-DD; you can guess from your turn timestamp>
tags: [<3-5 lowercase kebab-case tags relevant to this post; pick from: creator, recipe, automation, podcast, mac, prompt-engineering, game, mini-app, integration, behind-the-magic, ai-inbox, ai-calendar>]
series: <one of: Built in mChatAI, Recipe of the Week, Behind the Magic, SaaS Replacements, Founder Notes>
readingMinutes: <integer estimate>
---

<the polished post body, in markdown>

Polish rules:
- Fix awkward phrasing and trim filler.
- Strengthen the hook if it's weak.
- Convert any em-dashes to hyphens, smart quotes to straight quotes.
- Ensure every section's first sentence stands alone as a hook.
- If the post mentions an API key or paid service, add a parenthetical note about the free-tier path if one exists.

POST DRAFT:
{{INPUT}}
